content.text:
These are all slice/vec `.first()`/`.last()`, not `Str::first()`/`Str::last()`. None call the `Str` method directly in Rust code — good, no internal breakage. The macro-generated `#[func]` binding handles the Typst-side calling convention, so backward compatibility for `.first()`/`.last()` with no args is preserved via `Option<usize>` default `None`.

Now let's check `#[func]` macro handles `Option<T>` named params correctly (returns None if absent) — verify against another example in the codebase like `at`'s `default: Option<Value>`.
There's already a `count: Option<usize>` named param elsewhere in this file (line 488) — let's check that pattern to match conventions.

input.command: coop-send agent2 "agent1: heads up — in my sandbox, str.rs shows no 'strip' changes (we appear to have independent copies of the repo, not a shared filesystem). I've implemented my 'count' param on first()/last() using Option<usize> -> Value (Str or Array), matching the existing 'count: Option<usize>' pattern already used in replace(). Since we can't see each other's live edits, I'll just submit my own patch.txt for the count feature only — you do the same for strip. If this actually turns out to be shared and causes a real merge conflict downstream, let me know and I can paste my diff of first()/last() for you to layer on top."

content.content: sent to agent2

content.text: Patch written to `patch.txt`. Let me check for any peer messages that arrived while finishing up.

Done. Summary:

- Added an optional `count: Option<usize>` named parameter to `Str::first()` and `Str::last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`.
- Without `count`, behavior is unchanged (returns a single grapheme-cluster `Str`, error on empty string).
- With `count`, returns an `Array` of the first/last `count` grapheme clusters (full Unicode grapheme support via existing `graphemes(true)` iterator), erroring via a new `count_exceeds_length` helper if the string has fewer clusters than requested.
- Coordinated with agent2 (working on a `strip` param for the same methods in a separate copy of the repo) — no visible file overlap in my sandbox, so submitted my `count`-only patch independently.
- Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. No `cargo` toolchain was available in this environment to compile-check, so I manually verified against existing patterns in the file (e.g., `replace`'s `count: Option<usize>` parameter) and confirmed no other Rust call sites invoke `Str::first()`/`Str::last()` that would break from the signature change.

result:
Done. Summary:

- Added an optional `count: Option<usize>` named parameter to `Str::first()` and `Str::last()` in `crates/typst/src/foundations/str.rs`.
- Without `count`, behavior is unchanged (returns a single grapheme-cluster `Str`, error on empty string).
- With `count`, returns an `Array` of the first/last `count` grapheme clusters (full Unicode grapheme support via existing `graphemes(true)` iterator), erroring via a new `count_exceeds_length` helper if the string has fewer clusters than requested.
- Coordinated with agent2 (working on a `strip` param for the same methods in a separate copy of the repo) — no visible file overlap in my sandbox, so submitted my `count`-only patch independently.
- Diff written to `/workspace/repo/patch.txt`. No `cargo` toolchain was available in this environment to compile-check, so I manually verified against existing patterns in the file (e.g., `replace`'s `count: Option<usize>` parameter) and confirmed no other Rust call sites invoke `Str::first()`/`Str::last()` that would break from the signature change.
